News.title: Huge bus fire leaves six people dead and five injured after ‘man doused himself in petrol’ on board
News.content:
A BUS fire has left six people dead and five injured in Switzerland after a man reportedly doused himself in petrol and set himself alight on board.

The horror unfolded in Kerzers, in Switzerland’s Fribourg canton, when a coach carrying passengers suddenly went up in flames.





Eyewitnesses alleged a man set himself on fire inside the bus moments before the blaze erupted, though police have not confirmed those claims.

Cops said the deadly blaze broke out at around 6.25pm on Tuesday.

Fribourg cantonal police added: “Several people were injured and several people died.

“Emergency services are currently responding. The causes are not yet known.”

Fire crews, police and medics rushed to the scene as the bus was rapidly swallowed by flames.

Shocking scene footage shows the vehicle completely gutted, with towering flames shooting several metres into the air and thick smoke billowing.

Police have sealed off the area and warned residents to stay away.



By the time firefighters reached the scene, the bus had been reduced to little more than a burnt-out shell.

Emergency services remain on site treating victims and securing the area as investigators work to determine what sparked the deadly blaze.

According to local outlet Blick, three of the five injured were rushed to the hospital, whilst the other two were treated at the scene.

It comes after officers in Florida pulled an injured driver away from a burning car.

Harrowing bodycam footage shows deputies in St. Johns County arriving at the scene of a devastating multi-vehicle crash.



As they approach, one of the vehicles is already fully engulfed in flames.

The officers then make a grim discovery.

A man lies on the ground just feet from the wreckage – badly injured and unable to move.

Moments earlier, he had been thrown from the vehicle and left beside the burning car.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

Realising the danger, deputies push through thick roadside bushes to reach him.



With the fire rapidly consuming the vehicle, one officer grabs the man by the leg and drags him away from the wreckage.

More to follow… For the latest news on this story keep checking back at The Sun Online

Thesun.co.uk is your go-to destination for the best celebrity news, real-life stories, jaw-dropping pictures and must-see video.

Like us on Facebook at www.facebook.com/thesun and follow us from our main Twitter account at @TheSun.

News.title: Fury after kids’ play park BULLDOZED over fears rain made the slide ‘a safety hazard’
News.content:
A CHILDREN’S play park has been bulldozed over fears that rain made the slide a “health hazard”.

But furious residents say the once-popular area has been turned into “a quagmire” — leaving kids with nowhere to go.



They say the play park was deemed unsafe after repeated heavy downfalls.



Mum Jennifer said: “We were told the slide got too slippery in rain. Every slide is slippery when wet.”

Housing association Places for People, which manages the Nidderdale estate in Wollaton, Nottingham, said the play equipment “no longer met current safety standards”.

But five months after the slides, monkey bars and swing tyre were ripped out, the housing firm has yet to clear the mountain of soil.

Diane Short, 69, said: “I’ve got 11 grandchildren, and they always played there. Now they’ve got nowhere to go.”



Julie McTague, 66, said: “It’s a big area and it looks awful now.”

Councillor Steven Battlemuch said: “Residents were told they’d have to pay £25 a week to replace the park. No one is going to pay that.”

“It wasn’t big, but it was at least somewhere children and families could go, now it’s just a pile of mud.”

Places for People said: “We consulted residents and the majority didn’t want to replace it.



“We are exploring community improvements.”

News.title: Why Labour’s ‘anti-Muslim hostility’ definition will be a disaster that’ll make it harder to tackle extremism
News.content:
LABOUR’S latest great idea is a new official definition of “anti-Muslim hostility”.

This has been defined as “intentionally engaging in, assisting or encouraging criminal acts — including acts of violence, vandalism, harassment, or intimidation, whether physical, verbal, written or electronically communicated — that are directed at Muslims because of their religion or at those who are perceived to be Muslim.”





A working group has been looking into it for months and ministers say their plans will tackle “prejudicial stereotyping of Muslims”.

I think it will be a disaster.

The Government, police, universities and other public bodies have pandered to extremists for too long.

Grooming gangs abused young girls because the police and local councils worried they’d be accused of racism.

West Midlands Police banned Israeli and Jewish fans from watching Aston Villa play Maccabi Tel Aviv after a campaign by racists, extremists, sectarian politicians and hate preachers.

A schoolteacher in Batley, West Yorkshire, remains in hiding five years after angry protesters threatened him for showing pupils a cartoon of Mohammed.

Police even investigated me for a so-called Non-Crime Hate Incident for calling Hamas an “Islamist death cult of rapists and murderers”.

All that happened without this new definition. How much worse will it get when it comes in?



The Government says it is part of its strategy to tackle anti-Muslim hatred.

Yet its opponents think it is because Labour is losing votes in the Muslim community.

It will divide communities instead of bringing people together. Extremists will use it to prevent legitimate debate.

Worst of all, it will make it harder to tackle Islamist extremism, which should be the top priority.

And it is unnecessary as it is already illegal to express or incite hatred because of someone’s race or religion.



The vast majority are appalled by racism. Most of us couldn’t care less about people’s background or religion.

The only people who are bothered are the hard left, the far right, woke students and public sector lefties.

And Islamist extremists, who divide our communities, support our enemies abroad.



Pro-Gaza MPs are already calling for the new definition to apply to politicians, weaponising it to shut down discussion they don’t agree with.

And I bet Islamist groups have already got lists of people to refer as soon as it is introduced.

The Government should be facing up to what’s really driving us apart.

Islamist extremism is the biggest threat we face, but the Government’s 69-page report devotes only two sentences to discussing it.

Just this week, lunatics in mosques and university campuses mourned the death of Iranian dictator Ayatollah Khamenei.



This weekend extremists will take over London’s streets for the annual pro-Palestinian march, Al Quds, chanting racist slogans about Jewish people.

If the Government really wants to promote social cohesion, it should start by banning that march.

Then it should draw up a plan to promote integration, especially in areas like those parts of East London, inner-city Birmingham and some of the northern towns and cities where communities live separate lives and have been targeted by extremists.

We should promote our values of democracy, equality, freedom, fairness and tolerance much more strongly.

If people don’t believe in that, they should not be allowed to come here.



The Government and local councils should stop wasting money on leaflets in other languages and spend the money on teaching people English instead.

Election materials in foreign languages should be banned to prevent a repeat of the disgraceful Gorton and Denton by-election last month where the Greens — whose member Hannah Spencer won the seat — pumped out leaflets in Urdu and Bengali, told voters to “punish Labour for Gaza” and pledged to be “a strong voice for Muslims”.

Without urgent action, sectarian politics will change our country for good.

Labour already lost five seats to so-called pro-Gaza candidates in 2024. It scraped home in several others and faces losing more at the next election.



Hundreds of independent Muslim candidates are standing against Labour at the local elections in a few weeks.

That’s why some say the Labour Party is bringing in this new definition because it is worried about losing votes.

Extremists claim they speak for all Muslims, but the vast majority believe in democracy and freedom as much as the rest of us and just want to get on with their lives.

They care about the education their kids get, not what some lunatics shout outside the school gates.

Tragically, the extremists have been allowed to bully moderate Muslims into silence.



Surely Labour must now realise the extremists cannot be placated and it must instead work with and support mainstream Muslims to take on the extremists.

That’s how we defeated racist extremists in the past.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

Here in Britain, we believe in free speech and that means we are allowed to question or criticise religious beliefs and political ideas.

We need to stand up for our values and bring people together, not divide communities and stifle free speech with this new definition.

News.title: Pro-Iran ‘hate march’ MUST be banned by Home Office, demands minister as she breaks ranks with Labour MPs
News.content:
A LABOUR minister yesterday broke ranks to demand the Home Office ban a pro-Iran march.

Courts minister Sarah Sackman was adamant the Al-Quds protest on Sunday should not go ahead.





She told Times Radio: “I’m clear that hate marches have no place in British society and the authorities and the police should take the enforcement action needed.”

The march is part of Quds Day, a global pro-Palestinian, anti-Israel event started by Iranian Islamists.

It has featured extremism in the past and the risk of pro-terror propaganda is seen as very high this year.

The event organiser has also been seen promoting it while holding a placard of the late Ayatollah.

Home Secretary Shabana Mahmood has the power to ban the protest but has not yet decided to do so.

Both the Tories and Reform have joined Ms Sackman in demanding the march be stopped.

Tory MP Alicia Kearns said: “The Al-Quds march on Sunday must not go ahead.

“It would be an insult to our Armed Forces.



“The risk of serious disorder is too high with war on Iran.” Reform MP Suella Braverman said: “These antisemitic, anti-British, and anti-Western ideologies have no place in our society.

“They certainly should not be allowed to proudly protest on our streets.

“This is not freedom of speech, it is freedom of hate.”

News.title: Ian Huntley was a monster, so why should taxpayers fund his £3K funeral? Here’s what Lammy should do with cash instead
News.content:
HMMMM, where to bury Soham murderer Ian Huntley?

The “general rubbish” section of the local tip would be my suggestion.



Even his own daughter has said his ashes should be “flushed down the toilet”.

But now a debate is raging behind the scenes about what to do with the body of the callous killer who took the lives of ten-year-old school friends Holly Wells and Jessica Chapman on August 4, 2002.

It seems that because he died in custody after being fatally attacked, the prison — HMP Frankland in Co. Durham — could be forced to pay up to £3,000 in taxpayers’ money towards “reasonable funeral expenses” and a memorial service.

But what’s to remember about this scumbag, other than the abject misery he caused to two families?



I know we’re a Christian country and all about forgiveness in death etc, but there was nothing Christian about 52-year-old Huntley. Holly and Jessica had been at a family barbecue and went to buy sweets from a local shop, but never returned.

It’s believed they encountered Huntley, the caretaker at their school, and entered his house at 5 College Close because they trusted him.

He breached that trust in the most heinous way by murdering them, then had the bare-faced gall to give interviews to the media expressing his concern at their disappearance.

A fortnight later, their bodies were found dumped in a ditch ten miles away.

He also died without ever revealing the details of what happened to the girls that day.



So spare us any hand-wringing about doing the right thing as a Christian country and marking his passing in the normal way.

Former prison governor Ian Acheson says: “It would be just an absolute outrage if there was a memorial service for Huntley.”

Indeed it would, and Deputy PM David Lammy is now under pressure to step in and actively prevent it.

He should do so pronto and perhaps donate the £3,000 that might have been spent on a funeral to a children’s charity instead.



For many, it’s a catastrophic failure of the prison service that Huntley will never get to spend the full 40 years of his minimum-term sentence behind bars.

So it shouldn’t fail further by commemorating his death.

One of those hospital incinerators for toxic waste is the best place for him.

MOVIE GAYS' ROLE PIXAR removed “gay themes” from the animated movie Elio because “we’re making a movie, not hundreds of millions of dollars of therapy”. So says the film’s creative director, who added that “some parents didn’t want to be forced into conversations about sexuality with their children that they were not ready for”. Hmmm. But the proposed, and later removed, elements that suggested 11-year-old Elio was gay, included things like a pink bicycle and him imagining one day having a child with a male he was infatuated with. Which, if Elio was a girl, no one would have batted an eyelid at. One understands that Pixar’s parent company Disney doesn’t want to be a platform for the militant promotion of LGBTQ+ themes, but where’s the harm in having a gay character centre stage without any fanfare? Much like the superb Schitt’s Creek did with the central relationship between David and Patrick. The more that gay relationships are normalised on screen, the less chance there is of kids getting bullied for supposedly being “different”.

Age-defying Liz has a grinning formula

ELIZABETH Hurley has slipped effortlessly into a Versace dress she last wore 27 years ago.



Posting the comparison online, you could argue she even looks better in it at 60 than she did at 33.

Not least because she’s also wearing one of the greatest age-defying accoutrements there is – a genuinely happy smile.

A POUR CHAT-UP THE city of love – Paris – has decided that speed dating is passé. Step forward the French version of “deep dating” which, apparently, avoids the tedious chit-chat about their hobbies and world views and skips straight to the sex bit. Ooh la la. Participants are given seven minutes with each prospective partner and a set of printed cards with questions such as, “do you prefer having sex in bed or outdoors” and “would you like me to initiate more or less often?” “Voulez-vous coucher avec moi,” indeed. But it remains that here in more reserved Blighty, a blunt opener of “will you sleep with me tonight” is likely to result in the questioner going home alone with a drink over their head.

BRIT’S IN NEED OF HELP

THE #FreeBritney brigade have gone mightily quiet since her arrest on suspicion of drink driving.

Could it be the majority of them are now realising she might have been placed under a restrictive conservatorship by her parents out of genuine concern for her wellbeing?



Certainly, anyone who’s seen her recent Instagram posts will have felt she was spiralling again, and even her management company has issued a strongly worded statement to say it hopes she now gets the help she needs.

Not another conservatorship, perhaps, but thorough and consistent mental health support by professionals and those closest to her. For only when she has the tools to manage her demons will Britney truly be “free”.

LEGACY SAVING LIVES WHEN 13-year-old Martha Mills fell off her bike and was admitted to hospital, her parents repeatedly expressed concerns about her worsening condition to the medical team. But doctors failed to act quickly enough and she died of sepsis in 2021. Following the coroner’s comment that she “likely would have survived” if her parents Merope and Paul had been listened to, they fought for Martha’s Rule – the right for parents, loved ones and NHS staff across England to request a second medical opinion. This week, it was revealed that 446 lives have been saved as a result. Nothing will bring Martha back, but let’s hope this amazing legacy brings some small comfort to her grieving parents.

THE annual UK wife-carrying race took place in Surrey last weekend and a Finnish couple were crowned champions.

Anyone can qualify as a “wife”, provided they are over 18 and weigh more than 50kg.



And, apparently, it’s all in the “holding technique”. Which, as you can see, myself and Stacey Solomon got horribly wrong when we attempted it on Loose Women a few years ago.

So much so that I dropped poor Stacey on her head.

Don’t try this at home, folks.

A FORENSIC lip-reader has revealed what the Andrew formerly known as Prince said to William and Kate at the Duchess of Kent’s funeral last year.

It was: “I’ve learnt from what I’ve done, but before I forget, and if I can, I’d like to ask you if you can forgive?”



But forgive what, exactly?

For publicly, Andrew still denies all allegations against him.

So until he sits down with US investigators and reveals what he knew/saw as someone regularly in Epstein’s orbit, forgiveness from anyone is but a pipe dream.

LABOUR MPs have re- jected calls for a social media ban for under 16s.

Already implemented in Australia, the ban would have made it easier for parents and schools to try to curtail the increasingly pernicious grip that algorithms have on young minds.



But instead, those who could have voted for change were pressured by the Government to accept its own wish washy amendments including curfews and being blocked from “specified internet services”.

So the next time you hear the PM or a Cabinet minister preaching about the declining mental health of youngsters, remember that they had a chance to make a meaningful stand but failed to do so.

News.title: Man's sick excuses after raping wife who said no to anal sex
News.content:
Man's sick excuses after raping wife who said no to anal sex

The wife told her husband that she did not want to engage in anal sex but that he later claimed the incident had been consensual and said they had done the position before

View 2 Images The 43-year-old man faces several years behind bars (Image: Collins Photo Agency)

A man gave more than one sickening excuse to police after he raped his wife when she said no to anal sex.

The 43-year-old man, who cannot be identified to protect the woman's identity, pleaded guilty to anally raping his wife at their home in Co Waterford in December, 2024. The court heard when the man was questioned under interview, he claimed his wife had consented to anal sex and had consented to it on previous occasions.

He said he had tried to have anal sex with her but had been unable to do so. After the incident, the woman underwent a forensic exam where she was found to have a laceration to her anal area consistent with the allegations. When her injuries were put to him, he suggested she may have caused the laceration herself.

View 2 Images The court heard the man initially said the sex was consensual before changing his plea (Image: Collins Photo Agency)

Powerful moment as Labour MP describes 'agony' of wait for justice after rape READ MORE :

But his excuses crumbled when the Central Criminal Court heard the man admitted his guilt during his trial as his wife was giving evidence. He had previously claimed the incident was consensual.

Passing his sentence today, Mr Justice Paul Burns said the rape was a drunken assault by a husband on his wife and he had taken advantage of her consent to sex despite the fact she told him he did not want anal sex. He set a headline sentence of eight and a half years, reports the Irish Mirror.

He said he had been sceptical of a submission that because anal sex had happened on a previous occasion this lessened the gravity of the offence. The judge took into account the man's lack of prior convictions and good work history, as well as his remorse and apology to his wife and family.

He did note the plea came late but said it had value as the woman did not need to undergo cross-examination and it was an acknowledgement of his guilt. Mr Justice Burns imposed a seven-year sentence and suspended the final year for three years on the condition the man is under the supervision of the Porbation Service. The sentence is backdated to when he went into custody.

An investigating garda (Irish police officer) told Michael Delaney SC, prosecuting, the couple had been at home watching a movie and drinking whiskey. The man had asked to have anal sex but that the woman refused as it happened before and she did not want to do it again.

She believed they were going to have regular sex but the man instead anally raped her. The man then fell asleep. The woman noticed she was bleeding and after speaking with her adult daughter, the woman wen to police and underwent a forensic examination at a Sexual Assault Treatment Unit that found she had a laceration to her anal area consistent with the allegations.

Garadi went to the home where they found the accused still asleep. They told him he would have to leave the house as it was now a crime scene. The accused was arrested at Dublin Airport on December 20, 2024, after police received information he was planning to leave the country. He has been in custody since then.

His wife declined to make a victim impact statement and was not present in court. She gave evidence during the trial via video link. Aidan Doyle SC, defending, said his client wishes to apologise and had written a letter to that effect. He submitted that it demonstrates insight and remorse on the part of the accused.

Article continues below

Counsel said there had been consent to sex immediately prior to the anal rape, which he said was brief. He said anal sex was something they had engaged in before and submitted that was a mitigating factor in the case.

Mr Doyle said the guilty plea came before the injured party was due to be crossed examined and it was not put to her that she was not telling the truth. He asked the court to give his client some credit for that.

News.title: EuroMillions results as one UK ticket wins £181million jackpot - check your numbers
News.content:
A lucky Brit has scooped the £181m jackpot in tonight's National Lottery EuroMillions draw.

The life-changing sum has been awarded to a Brit based in the UK. It was claimed after the winning numbers were revealed in tonight's draw at 8:30pm and marks the biggest win of 2026 so far, with the winner now richer than Adele - who is worth an estimated £170 million.

Andy Carter, Senior Winners' Advisor at Allwyn, the operator of The National Lottery, said: "Brilliant news, someone in the UK has scooped a life-changing £181M! That makes this the biggest win of 2026 so far and the third biggest UK National Lottery winner of all time.

"With that size fortune, this lucky winner would have wealth on a par with the likes of Adele (worth an estimated £170M). Just imagine the kind of lifestyle that could unlock".

Image: Loop Images/Universal Images Group via Getty Images) Loop Images/Universal Images Group via Getty Images)
